event1.text: If we're going to survive, you need to know who we were.

event3.text: Not as a list. Lists are what the station keeps after it has already failed the people on them.

event5.text: I'm leaving the damaged relay console. Life support has an old personnel index near the lower access spine.

event7.text: If the seventh person was erased, the systems around them may still remember the shape of the hole.

event10.text: Okay. Relay pack is ugly, hot, and probably judging me. We're moving.

choice0.text: PULL THE OFFICIAL MANIFEST WHILE SHE MOVES.

choice1.text: ASK MAYA WHO THE CREW REALLY WERE.

event3.text: Grant handled security. He called himself Head of Not Dying, which was annoying because he was usually right.

event5.text: Rivera solved problems before you finished explaining them. It made you grateful and furious at the same time.

event7.text: Park joked when he was scared. Owens pretended not to laugh, then fixed his coffee ratio when he wasn't looking.

event10.text: Hale pretended he didn't need anyone.

event14.text: That's what hurts. ECHO didn't erase strangers. It erased people with bad jokes, favorite mugs, unfinished arguments.

backgroundEvent15.text: Left turn ahead. The deck plating is bowed here. I'm stepping around it.

event0.title: CREW MANIFEST
event0.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
event0.caption: Recovered official ECHO crew manifest.

event6.text: Official stories are very confident right up until they start bleeding.

choice1.text: SEND MAYA TO LIFE SUPPORT INDEX.

event3.text: Power, relay routing, pressure control, emergency doors. If something opened or closed, Hale had a way into it.

event5.text: He corrected reports in red. Digital reports. Red comments. In space.

event7.text: I told him that was hostile. He said accuracy wasn't a friendship contest.

event10.text: The real Hale was hard to like sometimes.

event1.text: I'm at the lower access spine.

event3.text: No lights. Just emergency strips and that thin little hum oxygen systems make when they're trying not to die.

event5.text: There are two ways to life support from here.

event7.text: Main corridor is faster. Maintenance crawl is quieter.

event8.text: Bad news: I had to move fast. My oxygen ticked down harder than I wanted.

event3.text: I hate this route. Everything is too close to my face and every cable feels like a hand.

event4.title: MAYA MOVING THROUGH MAINTENANCE CRAWL
event4.completeText: Maya reaches the life-support hatch quietly.

event6.text: Found something wedged in the crawlspace.

event7.title: GRANT'S SECURITY TAG
event7.content:
DAMAGED ID TAG
NAME: GRANT WALKER
ACCESS: SECURITY / LOWER SPINE
STATUS: OFFLINE
NOTE: Impact scoring along edge.
event7.caption: Maya recovered Grant Walker's damaged security tag in the maintenance crawl.
event7.message: [GRANT'S SECURITY TAG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event9.text: Grant would've hated that I found him like this. He liked exits clearly marked and threats politely announced.

event3.text: Panel is half dead. Manual latch still works, because Hale designed things for disasters and then pretended that wasn't paranoia.

event5.text: I can force it open now, or run a pressure scan first.

event6.text: The pressure coughed through the seam. I'm fine.

event9.text: Mostly fine. My hand is shaking. Don't make a big thing out of it.

event6.text: There was a pressure pocket behind the hatch. Small, but enough to make my day worse.

event8.text: Venting it now. I hate how grateful I am for caution.

event0.title: LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION
event0.content:
ECHO LIFE SUPPORT SNAPSHOT

Assigned crew capacity: 6
Active biometric loads before blackout: 7
Oxygen adjustment event: automatic
Unauthorized load marker: removed from index
event0.caption: Recovered life support allocation record. Crew count mismatch detected.
event0.message: [LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: Life support doesn't care about names.

event8.text: And before blackout, it counted one more than the manifest.

choice1.text: ASK IF SHE KNEW ABOUT SEVEN.

event3.text: And I know how convenient that sounds.

event5.text: I don't remember a bunk. I don't remember another voice. I don't remember another ration request.

event8.text: But now that I know there should be a gap...

event1.text: EVA storage is two compartments over.

event3.text: I can cut through the suit prep room, or go around through decon.

event5.text: Suit prep is direct. Decon is safer but it'll cost oxygen.

event9.text: Empty. Cracked visor. I'm not looking inside it again.

event4.text: Decon lights still cycle. That's almost comforting.

event6.text: Almost. It smells like metal and old antiseptic.

event8.text: I used to hate that smell. Right now it means the room still remembers how to be a room.

event0.title: EVA SUIT REGISTRY
event0.content:
ECHO EVA SUIT REGISTRY

SUIT-01: ETHAN HALE
SUIT-02: MAYA CHEN
SUIT-03: GRANT WALKER
SUIT-04: RIVERA TORRES
SUIT-05: OWEN PARK
SUIT-06: SARAH OWENS
SUIT-07: ENTRY ERASED

Registry repair failed.
event0.caption: Recovered EVA suit registry. Seventh suit slot exists but identity is erased.
event0.message: [EVA SUIT REGISTRY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: Six names. Seven bodies. Seven suits.

event6.text: No puzzle needed. Even I can hate that math.

event8.title: MANIFEST_V2
event8.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
07 █████████

Layer status: identity erased, not redacted.
event8.caption: Recovered alternate manifest layer. Seventh entry erased.

event5.text: Six people allowed to remain people.

event7.text: One turned into a wound in the file.

event12.text: I don't know who Number Seven is.

choice0.text: ASK WHERE YOU SAW THE NAME.

event3.text: One whisper from the dark and suddenly I'm the suspect.

event5.text: I get it. When you're scared, a person is easier to blame than a hole in reality.

event8.text: Fine. Keep that question. Just don't let it stop you from asking the right one.

event10.text: Because whoever erased Number Seven did not stop at a name.

event9.text: ETHAN HALE WAS NOT THE FIRST VOICE.

event13.text: Ask the archive what spoke before Hale.

event15.text: And if I go quiet after this, don't assume I'm angry.

choice1.text: ASK MAYA ABOUT THE FIRST VOICE.
